_comment: Schema example for Get-O365MailGroupInventory-Multi.ps1. Copy to tenants.config.local.json (gitignored) and populate with real values via scripts/Setup-LocalConfig.ps1. Default behaviour is to enumerate every GDAP customer; entries in the tenants array either (a) supply auth for a tenant that has no GDAP relationship, or (b) override the auth mode for one that does.
_v1Note: v1 — the single-tenant inventory script this wrapper drives — is NOT bundled with this repo. Install it separately and point v1ScriptPath at the .ps1 entry point. v1 must be a PowerShell 7+ script that accepts -OutputPath and produces a 16-tab .xlsx of M365 posture data; the wrapper either dot-sources it (library mode) or spawns a child process per tenant, depending on the wiring path you pick (see README).

partner._comment: Partner-center app registration in your home tenant. homeTenantId + clientId are ALWAYS required (used by both delegated and app-only auth modes). The cert fields below are required ONLY when running the wrapper with -AppOnly (cert-based unattended runs). Staff running in default delegated mode don't need a cert and can leave the cert fields null.
partner._certComment: Cert fields — required only with -AppOnly. Leave null on staff machines.
partner._pfxPasswordNote: When -AppOnly is set, the wrapper resolves the PFX password from the env var named here (so the password never lives in any file). Set it per shell session: $env:M365_MULTI_PFX_PASSWORD = 'your-password'  (PowerShell). Falls back to an interactive prompt if the env var is not set.
partner._setupNote: Run scripts/Setup-LocalConfig.ps1 to populate the real-config copy at tenants.config.local.json (gitignored). Use -AppOnly to also collect cert fields for unattended/scheduled runs.

defaults._comment: Applied per-tenant unless overridden in the tenant block.

exclude._comment: Tenant IDs to skip even if a GDAP relationship exists. Useful for sandbox / decommissioned customer tenants that still appear in delegatedAdminCustomers.

tenants._comment: Example 1 — GDAP-implicit. Most managed customers fit here. Auth uses the partner app + delegated tokens; nothing else needs to be supplied. The block exists only to override shortName, skip flags, or notes; if you don't need overrides, omit it entirely and let the GDAP enumerator pick the tenant up by default.

Example 2 — stored-creds-explicit. Tenant has no GDAP relationship and uses local admin instead. Credentials live in PowerShell SecretManagement; the script resolves them at run time and never reads the secret value into a log. NOTE: as of 2026-05-05 no UOTech customer uses this mode (every customer is on or moving onto GDAP). Kept here purely as a schema example for future contingency.

auth._caveat: Password-based auth requires a CA exemption (named-location or device filter) and an MFA bypass for this admin account, OR an in-tenant app registration with cert-based auth — see auth.mode=app-only-cert below for the cleaner alternative.

tenants._comment: Example 3 — app-only-cert. Tenant has its own admin app reg with cert-based credential. No GDAP relationship needed; the script connects directly with the in-tenant app. Cleanest non-GDAP option when the customer admin will install a cert.

Example 4 — interactive. Last resort: human at the keyboard, browser popup, MFA-aware. Useful for one-off runs against a tenant where neither GDAP nor a service account is available. The script will pause for the auth flow, then continue.
